extension_description.message: Type in Korean (Hangul) in your browser. Includes a romanisation tool and an on-screen keyboard.

romanize_popup_romanizedTextTextboxHint.message: The romanised version will appear here

gettingStarted_intro.message: Choose whether this extension should type Hangul in web pages, or stay out of the way while you use the other tools.

gettingStarted_question.message: How will you use this extension?

gettingStarted_choiceTypeHangul_label.message: Type Hangul in web pages with this extension

gettingStarted_choiceTypeHangul_descript.message: Enables Korean typing mode so the extension turns typed keys into Hangul in web pages.

gettingStarted_choiceOtherTools_descript.message: Disables Korean typing mode so the extension will not unexpectedly switch web-page typing into Hangul.

gettingStarted_noticeOtherTools.message: Korean typing mode is off. The extension will not switch web-page typing into Hangul. You can still use the other tools, and you can turn Korean typing back on later in Options.

gettingStarted_notice.message: Korean typing mode is enabled. Press the default toggle key (Right Command on Mac, Right Alt elsewhere) to turn Hangul typing on or off in the current tab. You can also click the extension icon.

gettingStarted_errorLoad.message: Settings could not be loaded. Open Options and try again.

gettingStarted_showIcon_chrome.message: Prefer a visible button? In Chrome, click the Extensions puzzle-piece icon, find Korean IME, then click the pin icon. Click the extension icon to switch between English and Hangul.

gettingStarted_showIcon_firefox.message: Prefer a visible button? In Firefox, click the extensions button, find Korean IME, then use its menu to pin it to the toolbar. Click the extension icon to switch between English and Hangul.

gettingStarted_showIcon_showVideo.message: Show me how to pin it

gettingStarted_showIcon_videoLabel.message: Video showing how to pin the Korean IME extension icon

gettingStarted_about_typingMode.message: Korean typing mode turns keyboard-layout input into Hangul.

gettingStarted_about_romanization.message: Romanisation tools convert existing Hangul to Latin letters.

gettingStarted_about_layoutExample.message: With the Korean Dubeolsik (두벌식) keyboard layout, dkssudgktpdy produces 안녕하세요.
